title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Manual Redirect Defense
summary: A webhook verifier encounters cross-host redirects, rewritten methods, and sensitive authorization headers while resolving a vendor migration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go manual redirect defense increment in a Go 1.26 net/http auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Strip or deliberately reissue authorization, cookies, origin-sensitive fields, and bodies when authority changes. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Manual Redirect Defense · Theory Manual Redirect Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model sends POST G-5 with authorization, then receives 302 ../moved, 307 https://upload.example.test/next, and a loop back to the first URL. Predict method, body, credential, origin, hop-budget, and SSRF decisions per step. Competency probe: Choose automatic, manual, or rejected redirects from method replay, SSRF, credential, audit, and user-consent risk. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
